Rule: Always lock camera angle before batch generation
Reference: Camera angle is the first constraint to define

Rule: Match tile size consistently
Why: Prevents tiny doors and giant barrels in same scene
Reference: Tile size defines scale for all props and characters

Rule: Character height = N tiles
Reference: Character must read clearly at intended display size

Rule: Set FOV before generation
Why: FOV affects perceived scale of environment

Rule: Lock camera height
Constraint: 1.7m human / 1.2m child / Eye level of character

Rule: Limit primary palette to 6-8 colors
column12: AI struggles with 10+ colors — style drifts
column13: Hard cap on palette size prevents inconsistency

Rule: Generate palette before any asset generation
Why: Reference-less generation leads to color drift
Reference: Palette is the foundation of visual consistency

Rule: Use off-black not pure black
Why: Pure black looks harsh on screens — off-blacks feel natural
Reference: Pure black is an AI tell

Rule: Validate color harmony with 60-30-10 rule
Lock light direction before batch
Reference: Lighting direction is as identity-defining as color

Rule: Use warm key + cool fill for moody scenes
Why: Creates depth and atmosphere without harsh shadows

Rule: Set shadow hardness per material type
Constraint: Hard for metal / Soft for organic / Medium for stone

Rule: Define ambient color and intensity
Use rim light on characters for silhouette separation
Why: Separates character from background without shadow complexity
Reference: Rim light is cheap depth cue

Rule: Flat shading with subtle noise texture
Reference: Grass is distant and ambient — high detail is wasted
Game_Style: Flat shading at game scale reads clearly

Rule: Matte with edge highlighting
Reference: Stoic and heavy — reflections break the material
Game_Style: Edge detection style for stylized stone

Rule: Reflective with environment tint + rim light
Why: Metal reads as metal through reflection and rim
Reference: Metal is defined by what it reflects

Rule: Flesh: subsurface scattering hint on edges
Why: Organic materials glow from within — dead flat looks fake
Reference: Subsurface hint at edges reads as alive

Rule: Cloth: no reflections
Why: Soft matte with weave pattern visible at macro
Reference: Fabric absorbs light — reflections look like wet cloth
Game_Style: Fabric weave pattern at macro scale

Rule: Transparent with Fresnel effect + caustics hint
Why: Water is defined by transparency and light play
Reference: Water reads through refraction and rim

Rule: Transparent with environment reflection + chromatic aberration
Why: Glass bends light — pure transparency looks like air

Rule: Define border style per game type
Constraint: Transparent border for tiles / Contour outline for chars / No outline for 3D

Rule: Match border thickness across batch
Handle tile seams invisibly
Why: Visible seams break immersion in tile maps

Rule: For character outlines: use contrast separation not line art
Constraint: Color shift or value shift between adjacent elements
Why: Outlines look hand-drawn and cheap at scale

Rule: Lock head-to-body ratio before character batch
Constraint: 8-head for heroic / 6-head for realistic / 4-head for chibi
Why: Proportion mismatch breaks visual consistency across characters
Reference: Head ratio is the primary character style cue

Rule: Scale facial features to head size
Why: Faces must read at intended display size
Reference: Facial feature ratios define species and emotion

Rule: Limit detail to readable-at-scale level
Constraint: No detail smaller than 2px at final render size
Why: Detail beyond readability is wasted rendering budget

Rule: Clothing reads as silhouette first
Why: Silhouette is the primary read at gameplay distance
Reference: Detail is for close-up cutscenes only

Rule: Maximum 4 visible fingers on game characters
Constraint: 5 fingers look child-like or weird at small scale
Why: 4-finger stylized hands read cleaner at scale

Rule: Open palm = 4 finger-widths maximum
Why: Finger crowding at small scales breaks anatomy

Rule: Match perspective grid for environment
Constraint: One-point for corridors / Two-point for corners / Three-point for hero shots
Why: Perspective mismatch between assets breaks scene
Reference: Environment uses same vanishing points as camera

Rule: Lock frame rate before animation batch
Constraint: 8fps for pixel art / 12fps for casual / 24fps for fluid
Why: Frame rate defines the animation budget and feel
Reference: Frame rate is the first animation constraint

Rule: Character animation frame count must be divisible by 2
Use keyframe pacing: slow-in slow-out
Constraint: Anticipation frame before action / Follow-through after

Rule: Every attack action needs anticipation frame
Why: Anticipation is what makes action readable

Rule: Impact frames: hold 2x normal duration
Why: Hold creates impact weight and power

Rule: Idle and walk cycles must loop seamlessly
Constraint: First frame = last frame OR positions match

Rule: Match output resolution to target platform
Why: Wrong DPI = blurry or tiny output

Rule: For pixel art: never upscale with smoothing
For 3D: match texture resolution to poly count
Constraint: 2K textures for hero assets / 1K for props / 512 for background
Reference: Over-res textures on low-poly = wasted VRAM

Rule: Use alpha channel for transparency
Constraint: PNG-32 for UI / TGA for game
Why: Premultiplied alpha preferred for engine import

Rule: Enforce naming convention
Include size in filename for sprites
Why: Size in filename = instant scalability info

Rule: Organize layers logically before export
Use platform-appropriate compression
Constraint: Web: WebP / Game: PNG / Mobile: ASTC / PC: BC7
Why: Compression choice affects quality and load time

Rule: Generate reference batch first (8-16 assets)
Why: Early errors compound in large batches

Rule: Score every asset on perspective
Severity: Score 1-5 on each dimension — reject below 4 on perspective or scale

Rule: Document successful parameters for next batch
Test assets in-engine before full batch
Reference: Preview in target engine before committing

Rule: Purple and blue neon glow is the universal AI aesthetic
Why: AI defaults to purple/blue neon — it reads as fake

Rule: Gradient text and buttons are AI defaults
Constraint: Avoid gradient fills — use solid colors with depth cues
Why: Gradients are the most common AI pattern
Reference: Flat color + shadow = cleaner than gradient

Rule: Generic proportions are AI safe defaults
Constraint: 8-head heroic for everyone / Same face syndrome
Reference: Define unique proportion ranges per character

Rule: AI over-uses warm ambient lighting
Constraint: Mix cool and warm zones — avoid all-warm scenes
Game_Style: All-ambient scenes look flat and fake

Rule: Skin tones cluster around beige/peach defaults
Game_Style: AI uses safe average skin tones
Severity: Range: pale blue-white to deep brown with undertones

Rule: 3 equal-width columns of cards = AI default layout
Centered hero section = AI default landing page
Why: Centered hero over dark image is overused

Rule: Inter font as default = AI generation
Circular spinner loading indicator = AI default
Constraint: Use skeleton loaders matching actual content layout
Why: Circular spinners are the laziest loading pattern

Rule: Pure black #000000 backgrounds = AI default
Fake round numbers (99.99%
Generic company names (Acme
Startup buzzwords in copy (Elevate
Reference: Use specific concrete verbs and metrics

Rule: Outer glow box-shadow = AI default styling
Constraint: Use inner borders or tinted shadows matching background hue
Why: Outer glows look cheap and pasted

Rule: Default shadcn/ui without customization = AI default
Gradient text headers = overused AI pattern
Game_Style: Gradient headers read as "trying to look premium"

Rule: Broken Unsplash random URLs in sample data
5-finger hands = AI safe default
Why: AI over-renders hands — 5 fingers look weird at scale

Rule: Same face syndrome across NPCs
Constraint: Define distinct face shape ranges per character type

Rule: Anatomy errors (extra fingers
Overly smooth skin textures
Constraint: Use painterly or stylized skin (no pore detail at game scale)
Why: AI renders photorealistic skin that looks out of place

Rule: Perfect symmetry in faces and poses
Perfect grass / uniform tree placement
Game_Style: AI places elements with mathematical uniformity

Rule: Uniform cloud/haze density
Constraint: Use gradient fog: dense at ground
Reference: AI defaults to uniform atmospheric effects

Rule: Over-detailed environment at distance
Constraint: Match LOD detail to camera distance
Why: AI renders everything at hero detail

Rule: Glass materials too clean / too reflective
Plastic-perfect materials
Set polygon budget per asset type before generation
Match texture resolution to display size
Merge static props into atlases
Constraint: Single atlas for all static props (max 2048x2048)

Rule: Limit shader complexity per platform
Constraint: Mobile: simple PBR / PC: full PBR + AO

Rule: Use sprite atlases for 2D
Cap particle count per effect
Generate LOD chain for all 3D assets
Use platform-native compression
Constraint: ASTC for iOS / BC7 for PC / DXT5 for Android

Rule: Validate palette with color blindness simulators
Game_Style: 10% of men are color blind — red-green is most common

Rule: WCAG AA minimum for UI: 4.5:1 for text
Constraint: Text must read on all backgrounds

Rule: Respect prefers-reduced-motion
Constraint: No looping animations for accessibility users
Reference: All animations must have reduced-motion mode

Rule: Icons must have text labels or clear affordance
Constraint: Don't rely on color or shape alone

Rule: Support 200% UI scale minimum
Why: Zoom support for low vision users

Rule: Every major generation creates a new version
3-tier approval: Concept → Draft → Final
Constraint: Each stage has checklist + named reviewer

Rule: Reject below threshold on any dimension
Why: Low scores get expensive in engine

Rule: Turn successful generations into rules
Clean exports before engine integration
Embed asset metadata in filename and/or sidecar JSON
Keep originals before any optimization or compression
Build reference board before starting production
Test in target engine before batch commitment
Build feedback loop: generation → review → refine → template
